question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with duplicate or conflicting HTTP headers that cause the middleware.Instrument to misidentify the request body size, resulting in requestBytesHistogram recording incorrect values and producing inaccurate records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to panic in a goroutine spawned by the handler (not recovered by MetricsMiddleware), causing the requestInflightGauge to never be decremented and producing a permanently incorrect inflight count in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 100 Continue responses, causing the middleware.Instrument to record incorrect timing in requestDurationHistogram because the duration measurement starts before the 100 Continue is sent, producing incorrect latency records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with an HTTP/2 protocol that causes the middleware.Instrument (designed for HTTP/1.1) to mishandle stream multiplexing, causing requestInflightGauge to over-count or under-count concurrent requests and producing incorrect inflight records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 206 Partial Content for range requests, causing responseBytesHistogram to record only the partial response size rather than the full response size, producing incorrect response size records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 304 Not Modified (empty body), causing responseBytesHistogram to record 0 bytes for those responses, and if this is unexpected, producing incorrect response size distribution records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests, and by analyzing the 'status_code' label in requestDurationHistogram exported to mirror nodes, infer the server's rate limiting thresholds and adjust attack frequency to stay below detection?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 503 Service Unavailable, and by reading the 'status_code' label in requestDurationHistogram at /metrics, determine when the server is under stress and time further attacks during degraded states?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the middleware.Instrument to record a negative duration in requestDurationHistogram (e.g., due to clock skew or NTP adjustment), producing invalid histogram observations that corrupt mirror node metric exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 200 OK with an empty body, causing responseBytesHistogram to record 0 bytes for those responses, and if this is unexpected, producing incorrect response size distribution records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 500 Internal Server Error with a large error body, causing responseBytesHistogram to record a large byte count for error responses, skewing the response size distribution in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the server to return HTTP 200 OK with a very large response body (e.g., a large block of transactions), causing responseBytesHistogram to record observations beyond the sizeBuckets max and producing incorrect response size distribution records exported to mirror nodes?
